summary: Iteration 2 (Settings + Subscription + due_date refactor + Free-tier gating). Backend: 36/38 pytest pass (13/14 new + 23/24 carried over). Frontend E2E (demo@debtwise.app): all 7 requested flows verified including Settings page, profile name save, Subscription plan cards with 'Save 17%' badge, monthly subscribe redirecting to checkout.stripe.com, mm/dd/yy display on debt cards, calendar popover allowing day 31 selection, free-limit-banner + Add-debt redirect to /settings?upgrade=1, simulator locked + upgrade button, ?upgrade=1 ring highlight on subscription section.

critical.issue: Returns 500 (InvalidRequestError: No such checkout.session) when polling status of a session that was just created via POST /api/subscription/checkout. Backend logs show emergentintegrations creates the session via the Emergent proxy host (integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe/v1/checkout/sessions) but get_checkout_status() then queries api.stripe.com directly with the proxy-only session_id, which Stripe rejects. This breaks the post-payment polling flow on the Settings page after Stripe redirect-back. Library-level inconsistency. Wrap get_checkout_status in try/except → return 'unpaid' fallback OR ensure the emergentintegrations StripeCheckout client routes status checks through the same proxy host as create_checkout_session.

minor.issue: Only triggered for historical records that still have a `due_day` field in the DB. New POST /debts (DebtPayload) silently drops any extra `due_day` field (pydantic ignores extras), so iteration-1 tests that seeded `due_day` no longer exercise this fallback. The fallback code itself is correct; just the iteration-1 test (test_debtwise_api.py::test_upcoming_returns_sorted) is now stale.

issues: Modal background is semi-transparent and the debt cards behind the dialog show through (visible in screenshot). Consider increasing the modal backdrop opacity or adding a stronger blur for better focus.

action_items: HIGH: Fix /api/subscription/status/{session_id} 500. Either (a) catch CheckoutError from emergentintegrations and return {payment_status:'unpaid', status:'open'} so the frontend polling loop can continue, or (b) configure StripeCheckout client to use the Emergent proxy for get_checkout_status too. Without this, after the user completes Stripe payment and is redirected back to /settings?session_id=..., the polling will crash and premium will never be granted client-side (webhook path is the only fallback).
LOW: Update iteration-1 test (test_debtwise_api.py::test_upcoming_returns_sorted and seeded_debts fixture) to use `due_date` (YYYY-MM-DD) instead of legacy `due_day`.
LOW: Tighten modal overlay opacity in Debts edit dialog.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/debts gate correctly checks is_premium(user) and FREE_DEBT_LIMIT (3) before insert; 402 detail message includes 'Free plan is limited to 3 debts' as expected.

DebtPayload.due_date is Optional[str] without regex validation — a malformed value (e.g. '2026-13-99') is accepted at write time and only fails inside /api/reminders/upcoming's datetime.fromisoformat. Consider validating with `pattern=r'^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$'` or `date` type.
is_premium() correctly handles tz-naive ISO strings by assuming UTC. Good.
/api/subscription/checkout writes a payment_transactions doc atomically before returning the Stripe URL — correct.
POST /api/webhook/stripe correctly returns 200 even on malformed body (logs warning) — prevents Stripe retry storms on bad payloads.
/api/subscription/cancel only clears `plan` but keeps `premium_until` — matches spec.
Frontend Settings.jsx ?upgrade=1 query param adds ring highlight + scrollIntoView — verified.
Free limit banner only shows when non-premium user has >=3 debts; correctly hidden otherwise.
Server.py is now 838 lines — borderline. Consider splitting subscription + stripe code into a separate module.
test_credentials: demo@debtwise.app / Demo@1234 (free tier, 3 seeded debts). admin@debtwise.app / Admin@123. Test users created during pytest are test_<uuid>@example.com / Passw0rd!.
seed_data_creation: Pytest created throwaway users and TEST_-prefixed debts on those users. The demo user's name was changed to 'Demo Renamed' during frontend testing of PUT /api/profile (cosmetic, can be reverted via the Settings UI).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/. Iter2 tests at test_subscription_and_debts_v2.py. The known stripe status 500 is a library-level routing inconsistency — emergentintegrations.create_checkout_session uses Emergent proxy host but get_checkout_status hits api.stripe.com directly. Webhook path is unaffected. Frontend testids: nav-settings, profile-name-input, profile-email-input, save-profile-btn, subscription-section, plan-monthly, plan-annual, plan-monthly-cta, plan-annual-cta, plan-annual-badge, debt-due (calendar popover trigger), debt-due-popover, free-limit-banner, simulator-locked, simulator-upgrade-btn. After clicking plan-monthly-cta the page navigates away to checkout.stripe.com — capture page.url before the redirect or listen for 'request' events with stripe.com substring.
rca_of_the_issue: Stripe status 500 RCA: in /app/backend/server.py the StripeCheckout client is constructed with api_key='sk_test_emergent' and a webhook_url. Inside emergentintegrations, create_checkout_session targets the Emergent proxy (https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe/v1) which creates a session in a sandbox account, returning a session_id like cs_test_a1U28DG1.... However get_checkout_status uses the underlying `stripe` SDK against api.stripe.com which has no such session, raising 'No such checkout.session'. Reproduction: POST /api/subscription/checkout {package_id:'monthly', origin_url:BASE_URL} then immediately GET /api/subscription/status/{returned_session_id} -> 500. Mitigation: wrap the call in try/except CheckoutError and return {payment_status:'unpaid', status:'open', amount_total: int(txn['amount']*100), currency: txn['currency']} so the frontend polling loop on /settings can continue without crashing; rely on the webhook to grant premium once payment completes.
